results.title: Saving the garden crop
results.creator_credit: Jack Tilles, Walter Scott and Chester Lindstrom
results.brief_description: Detailed description of what to do with various types of vegetables which you grow in your own home garden and may have too many to use or give away immediately, and want to sort for later use. Onions, which need to be kept dry, can be stored in the attic; when beans have been dried they are stored in glass jars or cans; for storing apples, squash, cabbage, pumpkin and root vegetables, detailed instructions are given and demonstrated in this film.

results.title: Saving Grace
results.creator_credit: Costa Botes, Larry Parr, Kahukura Films and New Zealand Film Commission
results.brief_description: When Grace, a homeless teenager whose emotional stability was shattered when her mother committed suicide, meets Gerald, an unemployed, compassionate carpenter, a tentative relationship develops between the two. Grace moves into Gerald’s house and their budding friendship gradually turns into love. Yet everything changes when Gerald reveals a stunning and incomprehensible secret— that he is the Son of God.

results.title: Save those teeth
results.brief_description: Emphasises the importance of proper cleansing and care of teeth and illustrates how teeth are affected by excessive use of refined sugar. Identifies the kind of bacteria that change sugar to acid in the mouth. Demonstrates the use of sodium fluoride solution in the prevention of tooth decay and prescribes specific rules to be followed in the care of teeth.

results.title: Save this life
results.brief_description: The back-pressure arm-lift method of artificial respiration is shown by an instruction to Boy Scouts The correct position in which to place the patent and the position of the rescuer in relation to the patient is explained and the slow rhythmic action required is demonstrated with step-by-step commentary .

results.title: Save the panda
results.creator_credit: Miriam Birch, National Geographic Society and WQED
results.brief_description: There may be no more than 1,000 pandas left in the world. As a result of this, scientists, sponsored by a joint China-World Wildlife Fund project, are attempting to learn more about these rare animals. This film examines the studies, being performed by leading panda experts, at the Wolong Natural Reserve in the People’s Republic of China.

results.title: Save our soil
results.creator_credit: Jagat Murari, M. Bhavnani and Documentary Films of India
results.brief_description: Surveys India’s efforts to conserve soil in an effort to meet the challenge of rain and flood. The indiscriminate cutting of trees, the unplanned cultivation of slopes and uncontrolled grazing, all helped to rob the country of thousands of acres of land that were once fertile. Counter-terracing, strip cropping, trenching, and above all, afforestation schemes are undertaken to restore the balance of nature.

results.title: Savage iron
results.creator_credit: Don Anderson and Department of Film Production
results.brief_description: Development of the iron ore at Savage River, north-western Tasmania, including construction of a new town, mill, ore slurry pipeline, pelletising plant at Port Latta and offshore loading facilities. Briefly shows the Yawata steel works near Osaka Japan, where the ore is processed.

results.title: Sausalito
results.brief_description: Utilising an imaginative rather than a realistic approach, the director Frank Stauffacher, one of the leading experimental film makers in the United States, seeks to capture the atmosphere of Sausalito, a small village on the shore of San Francisco Bay. The film is divided into two sections; Landscape and Song and uses slow motion, split screen, and superimposition techniques. It consists of fragments of visual and auditory experience such as the lapping of water on the shore, the creaking redwood of old houses.

results.title: Saudi Arabia: the oil revolution
results.creator_credit: Antony Thomas and Learning Corporation of America
results.brief_description: Probes the internal tension of Saudi Arabia, in transition since the oil boom of the mid 1960s and the reforms of King Faisal. Suitable for upper secondary level.

results.title: Saturday parade
results.brief_description: A candid camera study with mood music of the Cape Town Parade on a typical Saturday. The second hand stalls and flower market; street sweepers; the carillon; street artists and photographers; children; the “table-cloth” coming down on Table Mountains; a hamburger stand and a horse cab a night.
